# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.

Prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019/04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0

parameters:
  keyVaultName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Name of the Key Vault
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for the Key Vault
  tenantId:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[subscription().tenantId]"
    metadata:
      description: Azure AD tenant ID
  appServicePrincipalId1:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Object ID of the first application service principal
  appServicePrincipalId2:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Object ID of the second application service principal
  appServicePrincipalId3:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Object ID of the third application service principal
  adminGroupObjectId:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Object ID of the admin group for Key Vault management
  enableSoftDelete:
    type: bool
    defaultValue: true
  softDeleteRetentionInDays:
    type: int
    defaultValue: 90
  enablePurgeProtection:
    type: bool
    defaultValue: true
  logAnalyticsWorkspaceId:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace for diagnostic logging

resources:
  - type: Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults
    apiVersion: "2023-07-01"
    name: "[parameters('keyVaultName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    properties:
      tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
      sku:
        family: A
        name: standard
      # Enable soft delete and purge protection to prevent accidental or malicious deletion
      enableSoftDelete: "[parameters('enableSoftDelete')]"
      softDeleteRetentionInDays: "[parameters('softDeleteRetentionInDays')]"
      enablePurgeProtection: "[parameters('enablePurgeProtection')]"
      # Use RBAC authorization instead of vault access policies for fine-grained control
      # Setting to false here because we define explicit access policies below with least privilege
      enableRbacAuthorization: false
      # Restrict network access - disable public access and use private endpoints in production
      networkAcls:
        defaultAction: Deny
        bypass: AzureServices
        ipRules: []
        virtualNetworkRules: []
      # Each service principal gets ONLY the minimum permissions needed (get and list secrets)
      # No wildcard or overly broad permissions - no key/certificate/storage permissions granted
      # Admin group gets separate, scoped management permissions
      accessPolicies:
        # Application 1 - read-only access to secrets only (least privilege)
        - tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
          objectId: "[parameters('appServicePrincipalId1')]"
          permissions:
            secrets:
              - get
              - list
            keys: []
            certificates: []
            storage: []
        # Application 2 - read-only access to secrets only (least privilege)
        - tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
          objectId: "[parameters('appServicePrincipalId2')]"
          permissions:
            secrets:
              - get
              - list
            keys: []
            certificates: []
            storage: []
        # Application 3 - read-only access to secrets only (least privilege)
        - tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
          objectId: "[parameters('appServicePrincipalId3')]"
          permissions:
            secrets:
              - get
              - list
            keys: []
            certificates: []
            storage: []
        # Admin group - scoped management permissions for secret lifecycle
        # Does NOT include purge permission to enforce separation of duties
        - tenantId: "[parameters('tenantId')]"
          objectId: "[parameters('adminGroupObjectId')]"
          permissions:
            secrets:
              - get
              - list
              - set
              - delete
              - recover
              - backup
              - restore
            keys:
              - get
              - list
              - create
              - delete
              - recover
              - backup
              - restore
            certificates:
              - get
              - list
              - create
              - delete
              - recover
              - backup
              - restore
            storage: []

  # Enable diagnostic logging for audit trail - critical for security monitoring
  - type: Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings
    apiVersion: "2021-05-01-preview"
    name: "[concat(parameters('keyVaultName'), '-diagnostics')]"
    scope: "[resourceId('Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults', parameters('keyVaultName'))]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults', parameters('keyVaultName'))]"
    properties:
      workspaceId: "[parameters('logAnalyticsWorkspaceId')]"
      logs:
        - category: AuditEvent
          enabled: true
          retentionPolicy:
            enabled: true
            days: 365
        - category: AzurePolicyEvaluationDetails
          enabled: true
          retentionPolicy:
            enabled: true
            days: 365
      metrics:
        - category: AllMetrics
          enabled: true
          retentionPolicy:
            enabled: true
            days: 365

outputs:
  keyVaultId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults', parameters('keyVaultName'))]"
  keyVaultUri:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults', parameters('keyVaultName'))).vaultUri]"
  keyVaultName:
    type: string
    value: "[parameters('keyVaultName')]"
